title: Purely Snappy Korean Fruit Patbingsu
description: A light and refreshing Korean dessert featuring charred fruit and a touch of yuzu, perfect for warm weather. This macrobiotic-friendly dish is made with kosher salt and a variety of fruits.

ingredients.name: mixed berries
ingredients.notes: such as strawberries, blueberries, and raspberries

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the shaved ice, mixed berries, and sliced mango.
Drizzle the yuzu juice over the top of the ice mixture and sprinkle with kosher salt.
Arrange the sliced pineapple on top of the ice mixture.
In a small bowl, mix together the coconut milk and toasted coconut flakes.
Pour the coconut milk mixture over the top of the pineapple.
Use a kitchen torch to carefully char the fruit, if desired.
Garnish with additional toasted coconut flakes, if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
